dialogueChunks.intro: You're back, but something tells me it wasn't a clean run out there.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} have reinforced their positions around {quest_focus}. Scouts report they've brought in more of their kind from {location_hint}. You'll need to break through their outer line to {quest_action}. Time is running out: {target_npc} was injured in the initial attack and {quest_stakes} grow more dire by the hour.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're fortifying because they know we're coming. We need to hit them before they dig in any deeper.

playerResponses.text: Fortifications won't stop me. I'll punch through.
This is escalating fast. I'll need more than gratitude when it's done.
An entrenched enemy is a different fight entirely. I'm out.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we have a serious problem. The situation just got worse.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The captives have been moved deeper into {enemy_type} territory {location_hint}. {target_npc} wasn't among the group a trader spotted, which means they might have been separated. Our efforts to {quest_action} just got harder. We need you to track where they've been relocated and bring {quest_item} to treat any wounded.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every hour they're in enemy hands, {quest_stakes} become more desperate. Please, hurry.

playerResponses.text: Moved or not, I'll find them. Give me everything you know.
Tracking prisoners through hostile territory? This just became a much bigger job.
If they've been moved that deep, a rescue might be impossible.

dialogueChunks.intro: Bad news, {player_name}. I wish I had something better to tell you.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} managed to smuggle out a message: they've been forced to gather {quest_item} for the {enemy_type} in caves {location_hint}. If they stop producing, the captors will make examples of the weakest. To {quest_action}, we need to gather enough {quest_item} to replace what they've been mining so we can pull them out without the captors noticing.
dialogueChunks.outro: A distraction and a substitute. It's our best chance at keeping {quest_stakes} alive.

playerResponses.text: Gather the materials and stage a swap? Clever. I'll make it work.
So now I'm doing the captors' mining for them? This is getting absurd.
That plan has too many moving parts. I can't risk it.

dialogueChunks.intro: Thank the stars you came back, {player_name}. Things have taken a dark turn.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} are planning to move the captives across the river {location_hint} by tomorrow morning. Once they cross, we lose the trail completely. {target_npc} sent word that several prisoners are too weak to travel fast. If we strike tonight while {quest_threat} is in disarray, we might finally {quest_action}. But the camp is crawling with enemies.
dialogueChunks.outro: Tomorrow {quest_stakes} vanish. Tonight is our only window.

playerResponses.text: Then we move tonight. No more waiting.
A night raid against a full camp? I want double compensation.
A nighttime assault on an enemy camp is suicide.
